summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of an electrifying NYE ’84 show highlighted by a monster Shakedown Street and fiery second‑set gems.
review: The recording is praised for its crisp, balanced, “big‑fat” sound that makes the band feel live and immediate. Musically the concert shines with a commanding Jerry Garcia, tight Brent Mydland keyboards and Phil Lesh’s booming bass, delivering standout moments like Shakedown Street, Scarlet > Fire, Peggy‑O and an inspired second set featuring Jack Straw → Bird Song, Space and Uncle John’s Cabin. Overall fans consider it one of the best New Year’s Eve performances of the mid‑80s.

ai_rating.rationale: All 12 reviews are 4–5 stars, overwhelmingly praising both performance and sound quality; the weighted average yields a rating in the high‑four range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clean, balanced mix with big‑fat low end; occasional crowd noise noted on Sugar Magnolia but overall live feel is superb.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set bursts with energy and tight grooves; second set goes primal, building momentum through seamless transitions (Jack Straw into Bird Song) and culminating in a soaring jam suite of Space → Wheel → Uncle John's Cabin.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Described as ‘in command from the first song’, delivering a ferocious Shakedown solo, emotive vocals on Peggy‑O and an intense Baby Blue; fans note his “happy” tone and powerful improvisations.
band_member_comments.Phil: Praised for a solid, primal groove, frequent “bombs” in Scarlet, Space and Wheel, filling out the bottom end with authority.
band_member_comments.Bob: Noted for tight rhythm work, contributions to Spanish‑jam sections of Space, and full‑harmony vocals on Uncle John’s Cabin.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland is highlighted as being “in fine form”, providing strong organ/keyboard textures and vocal harmonies (especially on Peggy‑O). Later comments reflect nostalgia for his era after his passing.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill and Mickey lock into a primal, driving beat; marimba‑like textures in Space and relentless propulsion on Jack Straw are singled out.
